name: Heights Finance
description_short: Heights Finance offers installment personal loans up to $10,000 at physical branch locations across 15 states, with same-day or next-day funding and flexible underwriting beyond credit scores.
description_long: Heights Finance is a regional installment lender operating physical branch locations across the Southeast, South, and Midwest United States. The company has been providing personal loans to consumers who may not qualify for traditional bank financing, with a stated focus on treating customers with respect and considering factors beyond credit scores. Their service model emphasizes direct interaction at brick-and-mortar locations, allowing customers to apply in-person with staff assistance. The company maintains a 4.7 TrustScore based on 21,518 customer reviews, suggesting consistent satisfaction with their service delivery. Heights Finance offers personal installment loans up to $10,000, positioning themselves in the mid-range of personal loan products. They serve customers seeking quick funding for various needs, with advertised fast approval and funding. Beyond basic personal loans, they provide credit insurance products (Silver Safeguard), home and auto financing options, and a referral program offering $30 bonuses. Their digital offerings include a mobile app for loan management and payments, though their core business remains branch-based. The company distinguishes itself through emphasis on personalized customer service, staff training visible in positive reviews mentioning individual employees by name, and operational flexibility—such as transitioning to cash-free payment systems while maintaining accessibility. Their stated philosophy positions them as alternatives to traditional lenders for consumers with imperfect credit histories. An honest assessment reveals Heights Finance operates within the installment loan sector where interest rates, though likely compliant with state lending laws, may exceed traditional bank offerings. The company's regional footprint (15 states) creates accessibility for some but excludes others entirely. While customer service reviews are notably positive, the absence of published APR ranges, fees, or specific loan terms on their website prevents transparent rate comparison—a common limitation in this sector.
pros: Fast funding advertised (check in hand within 24 hours in documented customer example)
Loan amounts up to $10,000 for mid-sized financial needs beyond payday loan limits
Operates 15+ state locations with physical branch locations for in-person service
4.7 TrustScore from 21,518 verified customer reviews with consistent positive feedback
Underwriting considers factors beyond credit scores, stated as welcoming to non-prime borrowers
Multiple payment options including mobile app and automated payments
$30 referral bonus program for customer acquisition
Cash-free branches with alternative payment methods to accommodate customer preferences
cons: No APR ranges or fee structure published on website, making rate comparison impossible
Limited geographic availability (15 states only) excludes significant portions of the country
Requires branch visit for in-person application; no fully online origination mentioned
Installment loan products typically carry higher rates than traditional bank personal loans
Limited transparency about credit requirements or qualification criteria on public website
best_for: Consumers in covered states with damaged credit seeking mid-sized loans ($3,000-$10,000)
Borrowers needing same-day or next-day funding who can visit a physical branch
Customers preferring in-person service and staff guidance through loan process
Individuals seeking debt consolidation or major purchases without traditional bank approval
diagnosis: Heights Finance is best for consumers in their 15-state service area with less-than-perfect credit who need $3,000-$10,000 quickly and prefer human interaction. The main caveat is the absence of published rates and terms online—customers must visit branches to learn actual APRs and fees, and rates will likely exceed traditional bank personal loans, making rate shopping essential before committing.
services: Mobile app for loan management and payments
In-person loan applications at branch locations
Customer support via phone and in-branch
